scope: CSB save-header identity and DM-vs-CSB save routing source-lineage only.

source_checks.claim: DM and CSB save headers are distinct source structures with explicit format IDs.
DM, CSB prison, and CSB game dungeons have separate source constants.
DM and CSB save-file IDs are separate in the source namespace.
Dungeon validation reads DungeonID through the CSB header layout when the save format is CSB.
Criteria 2 accepts CSB game only; criteria 3 accepts CSB prison or DM, not CSB game.
New Adventure creation is gated through F7272 criteria 3.
The SU1E-specific gate accepts only CSB-format saves whose DungeonID is CSB game.
Save output routes DM to DMSAVE.DAT and CSB game/prison to CSBGAME.DAT by DungeonID.
Saved-game load recovers DungeonID from the save header and documents the old fallback mismatch for actual CSB.

non_claims: No Firestaff runtime code was changed or validated by this gate.
No CSBGAME.DAT sample save is required or claimed present on N2.
No renderer, gameplay, or binary save compatibility parity is claimed.
No deprecated remote source, external capture, or non-N2 reference is used.
